facts.claim: 41 rules: dose suspension state machine, admin check/cancel/revert, fluid-balance ml export, 07:00 day-boundary, permission-gated controls, pharmacist forms.
Zero scoring rules; operational/UI/workflow logic for a home-care MAR, not ICU vitals/scoring.
Dominant theme: dose state (administrado/suspenso/motivo/can_delete) via strict branch-order trees + permission checks, duplicated across offline/online serializers and v1/v2 PDFs.
07:00 server-local-time day-boundary defines home-care 'day' for fluid-balance export (RULE-004) and dose display sort (RULE-005); not encounter/bed timezone aware.
qtd_exportada (ml) at dose-check feeds fluid-balance Entrada ledger; summation outside this partition.
Pharmacist sub-theme (RULE-019,027,037-040): reconciliation, antibiotic tracking, risk assessment, prophylaxis, 33-option intervention vocab; free-text, no thresholds.

Type: validation=14, workflow=9, decision-tree=8, threshold=5, eligibility=5. Category: care-pathway=17, data-validation=9, scheduling-operational=8, triage-eligibility=3, alert-threshold=2, billing-administrative=1, physiological-calculation=1.
Verdict: NOT_APPLICABLE=40, UNVERIFIABLE=1. No VERIFIED/clinical-DISCREPANCY verdicts — operational/UI rules, not clinical calculators.

constraints.text: PT-BR reason-for-non-administration vocabulary MUST be preserved verbatim: recusa_paciente/'Paciente recusou a administração', medicacao_suspensa/'Medicação suspensa pelo médico', nao_tem_farmacia/'Em falta na farmácia', outros/'Outros' (FE-only, unsupported by legacy BE enum).
v2 fluid-balance/dose-export design MUST replace server-wall-clock 07:00 boundary with explicit encounter/bed-timezone-aware policy (ADAPT), not port datetime.now().astimezone() as-is.
Checador-only cancel/revert authorization and can_manage_prescricao gating are load-bearing invariants, MUST be preserved.

load_bearing_rules.what: Per-dose suspension vs DT_SUSPENSAO; dead inverted-logic shadow methods
Order-level now()-based suspension disagrees in scope with dose-level check
07:00 boundary decides which day a dose's ml export books into fluid balance
4-branch state machine for administer/cancel/reschedule; drives signature+AMH Docs resend
Daily eligibility materializing today's continuous prescriptions from Tasy data
Delete authorization diverges: online returns None on missing context, offline reads precomputed flag
Bulk checkoff forces administrado=True, no txn wrapper, mutates shared dict in loop
Non-admin reason enum diverges FE(4)/BE(3); BE keyless lookup can raise KeyError
Only the checking user may cancel a scheduled dose (server-enforced)

systemic_issues.issue: Dead/shadowed code: duplicate get_suspenso methods, inverted logic, unreachable via method-shadowing
Cross-scope disagreement: header (now-based) vs dose-level (scheduled-day) suspension flags can contradict
Timezone/window bug: 07:00 boundary uses server wall-clock; unguarded .id access if no BalancoHidrico row exists
Cross-stack enum drift causing a live KeyError crash path in legacy v1 PDF rendering
Dead validation: validar_motivo guard pre-satisfied by its own call site; validar_alteracao_dado never invoked in-partition
Persisted enum typos incl. likely allergy/'alegria' mistranslation in 33-option vocabulary

motivo_nao_administrado.outros: Outros (FE-only; unsupported by legacy BE enum)

open_questions: RULE-PRESCRICAO-036 (checagem lock) call-site outside this partition unconfirmed.
Whether v2 keeps a 07:00 home-care day-boundary or normalizes to encounter-timezone day (ADR-001 precedence).
